title: Wayzata Police Department, MN - Flock Audit (Condensed)

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Wayzata Police Department, MN - Flock Audit (Condensed)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) User account audit logs documenting each time a department account has conducted a search or taken another action on the department's Flock Safety ALPR system from 1/1/2025 until the date this request is processed. Flock audit logs are retained indefinitely and contain metadata including, but not limited to: the date of the search, the time of the search, the search term/rules, and the user conducting the search. The audit logs are recorded in a database so I ask that you provide it to me as a .csv, .xls, or other database filetype.
--
2) Audit logs documenting the access reason for each time a department account has conducted a search on the department's Flock Safety ALPR system from 1/1/2025 until the date this request is processed. The access policy of Wayzata PD for the Flock Safety ALPR system requires a "valid reason". If this valid reason is not recorded anywhere, please indicate as such. If there are audit logs with information regarding ongoing cases and therefore they cannot be provided, please indicate the count of the logs not provided for that reason, and provide the remaining logs that do not have information regarding ongoing cases. Otherwise, if logs contain information that cannot be publicly shared, please redact the information, and provide a reason for the redaction.
--
3) Audit logs for any other departments who ran queries against Wayzata PD's Flock Data.
--
4) The names of all the agencies with which data is shared with through the Flock Safety ALPR system from 1/1/2025 until the date this request is processed
--
5) If any exist, please provide all agreements will local homeowner associations or private business or individuals for access to Flock Safety systems.
--
6) Correspondence (emails, letters, memoranda) between SFPD personnel and Flock personnel relating to the purchase, installation, or usage of the ALPRs. Flock's email domain is "flocksafety.com". You may de-duplicate emails as needed in case Flock sends out newsletters or emails to multiple Wayzata PD members. Please search back one year from the date of completion of this request. 

In the event that you determine certain records or portions of records are exempt from disclosure, I ask that you provide an explanation of the records or portions of records being withheld and the statutory exemptions under which they are being withheld. I have already reviewed the Flock Transparency Page for Wayzata Police: https://transparency.flocksafety.com/wayzata-mn-pd

I am requesting these records as a journalist in order to write articles in the public interest.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

communications.communication:

- Any other records including electronic records, audiotapes, videotapes, and photographs, as well as letters, emails, facsimiles, telephone messages, voice mail messages and transcripts, notes, and minutes of any meetings

In the event some portions of the requested records are properly exempt from disclosure, please disclose any reasonably segregable non-exempt portions of the requested records. If it is your position that a document contains non-exempt segments, but that those non-exempt segments are so dispersed throughout the document as to make segregation impossible, please state what portion of the document is non-exempt, and how the material is dispersed throughout the document. If a request is denied in whole, please state specifically that it is not reasonable to segregate portions of the record for release.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

title: CPRA Request – Records Referencing Corey Feldman (Jan 1, 2016 – Dec 31, 2025) (Ventura County Sheriff's Department)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act (Gov. Code § 6250 et seq.), I request copies of records from January 1, 2016 through December 31, 2025 reflecting any police reports, incident reports, investigative files, complaints, arrest records, or related documentation in which Corey Feldman is identified as:
- A named suspect;
- A person of interest; or
- A named subject of a complaint involving allegations classified as sexual assault, sexual battery, rape, or other sexual misconduct.

If responsive records involve allegations of sexual misconduct, I request redacted versions sufficient to confirm:
- Date of report;
- General classification of allegation;
- Disposition status (e.g., open, closed, unfounded, referred, etc.);
- Assignment to any investigative or specialized unit, if applicable.

This request is made for journalistic purposes and is not an assertion of wrongdoing, but an effort to determine whether official complaints were filed and how they were processed.

If any portion of the requested records is exempt from disclosure, please provide all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions and identify the specific exemption relied upon for any withheld material.

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Frederick Wiseman (January 1, 1930 – February 16, 2026)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records mentioning, relating to, or generated as a result of investigation or inquiry into the filmmaker Frederick Wiseman (January 1, 1930 – February 16, 2026). His death is widely-known and widely reported; as such, privacy concerns under FOIA Exemptions 6 and 7 are voided, as are any concerns under the Privacy Act. For example, you may find his New York Times obituary at the following link: https://www.nytimes.com/2026/02/16/movies/frederick-wiseman-dead.html

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records of both HQ and all field offices for all relevant names, agencies, organizations, companies and events including but not limited to those cited in the previous paragraphs and/or links as well as a cross-reference with the Southern Poverty Law Center to include any information provided by the SPLC. My request includes but is not limited to 137, 157, 176, 177, 183, 184, 188, and 214 files. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

If any part of these documents are exempt from disclosure, please be sure to review and release all reasonably segregable nonexempt portions of responsive documents, as the FOIA requires.

I request that I be properly classified as a media requester. I am an associate editor at Reason Magazine who has written numerous articles on topics of public interest for the Daily Beast, the Asia Times, Responsible Statecraft, the National Interest, America Magazine and the Armenian Weekly, and I am requesting these documents in an editorial capacity. Please contact me if you have any questions about my media status.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

title: Enforcement of Plate/Tab Laws during Metro Surge - Hopkins PD

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Enforcement of Plate/Tab Laws during Metro Surge - Hopkins PD
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

In light of recent public reporting and video documentation showing federal immigration personnel operating vehicles in the Twin Cities without visible license plates, with expired registration tabs, or in apparent violation of Minnesota vehicle statutes, I am seeking data to better understand how your agency enforces these laws uniformly within its jurisdiction, including whether such statutes are applied consistently regardless of the driver’s federal affiliation. The information we are requesting is not available on your Community Crime Map. We are requesting:

1. Summary Enforcement Data

Please provide a monthly summary (January 1, 2025 through present) of citations and warnings issued for the following violation types:
- Expired registration tabs
- No license plates
- Improperly displayed plates
- Switched or fictitious plates
- Operating a vehicle without valid registration

These may include (but are not limited to) the following statutes: Minn. Stat. § 168.09 – Registration / Display of Registration Plates, Minn. Stat. § 168.04 – Display of License Plates, Minn. Stat. § 169.79 – License Plates; Display Requirements, Minn. Stat. § 168.12 – Registration Tax / Tabs

For each statute listed above, please provide:
- Total citations issued (monthly totals)
- Total warnings issued (monthly totals - if available)
- Whether citation resulted in arrest (Y/N summary)
- Any internal code used for these violations
- Please do NOT requesting any personally identifiable information
----------

2. Stops or Citations Involving Federal Law Enforcement Personnel

For the same date range, please provide:
- The number of traffic stops conducted by Hopkins PD involving drivers identified as federal law enforcement personnel (if tracked)
- The number of citations or warnings issued to federal law enforcement personnel for the statutes listed above
- The number of such stops that resulted in no citation or warning
- If identity of agency (e.g., DHS, ICE, FBI) is tracked in any field, please include aggregate totals by agency.
- No names or personal identifying data are requested.

If no responsive records exist, please confirm that fact.
----------

3. Policies and Guidance

Please provide copies of:
- Current Hopkins PD written policies regarding enforcement of vehicle registration and license plate violations.
- Any written policy, directive, or standing order addressing enforcement discretion for government-owned or federal vehicles.
- Any temporary directives or operational guidance issued during Operation Metro Surge that address traffic enforcement priorities. This may include patrol briefs, SOPs, or other temporary directives issued regarding enforcement of plate/tab laws. Please include any memos, emails, powerpoint slides, or other documentation regarding temporary guidance for these vehicle violations since Operation Metro Surge began. 

Thank you in advance for the assistance. If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific statutory exemption relied upon. Let me know if I can provide additional context to aid in your search.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

title: Agreements and Technical Policies regarding Search Engine Indexing (UPF)

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Agreements and Technical Policies regarding Search Engine Indexing (UPF)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to Article I, section 24 of the Florida Constitution, and Chapter 119, Florida Statutes, I am requesting an inspection or copies of all public records regarding the relationship, agreements, and technical arrangements between the University Press of Florida (UPF) and both Google LLC (including Alphabet Inc.) and Microsoft Corporation.

Specifically, I am seeking records concerning the indexing of academic content by their respective web crawlers (e.g., Googlebot, Google Scholar, Bingbot) and the technical mechanisms used to bypass UPF subscription paywalls.

Please provide the following:

1. Contracts and Agreements: All current, active contracts, Memorandums of Understanding (MOUs), or formal agreements between UPF and Google, and between UPF and Microsoft, concerning the crawling, indexing, or full-text access of paywalled scholarly articles.
2. Technical Access Policies: Any internal policy documents, technical guidelines, or manuals detailing how UPF configures its servers or security systems to allow Google and Microsoft bots to bypass standard paywalls or security challenges (e.g., IP whitelisting protocols, "First Page" access rules, or specific metadata provision).
3. Data Sharing: Any documentation, including data-sharing addendums, outlining what user data, traffic analytics, or usage statistics UPF provides to Google or Microsoft in exchange for indexing services.


If you contend that any portion of these records is exempt from disclosure, please provide a written statement specifying the exact statutory citation for each exemption claimed, as required by Section 119.07(1)(c), Florida Statutes.

I remind the University that under Florida law, an agency cannot enter into a contract for the maintenance of a public records database that impairs the public's ability to inspect those records (Section 119.01(2)(c)). Furthermore, if a record contains both exempt and non-exempt information, the non-exempt portions must be redacted and released; a blanket denial is a violation of the Act.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Michael Veale

title: Agreements and Technical Policies regarding Google and Microsoft Indexing (U-M Press / Fulcrum)

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: Agreements and Technical Policies regarding Google and Microsoft Indexing (U-M Press / Fulcrum)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting records relating to the relationship, agreements, and technical arrangements between the University of Michigan Press (including the Fulcrum publishing platform) and both Google LLC (including Alphabet Inc.) and Microsoft Corporation.

Specifically, I am seeking records concerning the indexing of academic content by their respective web crawlers (e.g., Googlebot, Google Scholar, Bingbot) and the bypassing of U-M Press and Fulcrum subscription paywalls.

Please provide the following recorded information:

1. Contracts and Agreements: Copies of any current, active contracts, Memorandums of Understanding (MOUs), or formal agreements between U-M Press/Fulcrum and Google, as well as with Microsoft, concerning the crawling, indexing, or full-text access of paywalled scholarly articles and books.
2. Technical Access Policies: Any internal policy documents, technical guidelines, or manuals detailing how U-M Press and the Fulcrum platform configure their servers or security systems to allow Google and Microsoft bots to bypass standard paywalls or CAPTCHAs (e.g., IP whitelisting protocols, "First Page" access rules, or specific metadata provision).
3. Data Sharing: Any documentation outlining what user data, traffic analytics, or usage statistics (if any) U-M Press or Fulcrum provides to Google or Microsoft in exchange for these indexing services.

If you intend to apply exemptions under MCL 15.243 (such as those relating to trade secrets or commercial information), I ask that you consider the following:

Duty to Separate Exempt and Non-Exempt Material: Under MCL 15.244, if a public record contains both exempt and non-exempt material, the public body must separate the exempt material and make the non-exempt material available. The technical policies outlining how U-M Press interacts with search engine bots are matters of significant public interest and do not constitute exempt commercial secrets. I am happy to receive redacted documents if they contain genuinely protected financial pricing structures.

Public Interest over Commercial Secrecy: The University of Michigan Press acts as a major hub for academic dissemination. The terms under which it allows private, monopolistic tech companies backend access to publicly funded research is a matter of profound public interest that outweighs general claims of commercial sensitivity.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael Veale

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Agreements and Technical Policies regarding Google and Microsoft Indexing (UT Press)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting records relating to the relationship, agreements, and technical arrangements between the University of Texas Press (UT Press) and both Google LLC (including Alphabet Inc. and its subsidiaries) and Microsoft Corporation.

Specifically, I am seeking records concerning the indexing of academic content by their respective web crawlers (e.g., Googlebot, Google Scholar, Bingbot) and the technical mechanisms used to bypass UT Press subscription paywalls.

Please provide the following recorded information:

1. Contracts and Agreements: Copies of any current, active contracts, Memorandums of Understanding (MOUs), or formal agreements between UT Press and Google, as well as UT Press and Microsoft, concerning the crawling, indexing, or full-text access of paywalled scholarly articles.
2. Technical Access Policies: Any internal policy documents, technical guidelines, or manuals detailing how UT Press configures its servers or security systems to allow Google and Microsoft bots to bypass standard paywalls or CAPTCHAs (e.g., IP whitelisting protocols, "First Page" access rules, or the provision of specific metadata tags).
3. Data Sharing: Any documentation outlining what user data, traffic analytics, or usage statistics (if any) UT Press provides to Google or Microsoft in exchange for these indexing services.

Pre-emptive arguments regarding TPIA Exceptions:

I anticipate that you may consider applying exceptions to disclosure, such as Section 552.104 (Information Related to Competition or Bidding) or Section 552.110 (Trade Secrets). I ask that you carefully consider the following:

Duty to Redact: The fundamental parameters of how a public university grants search engines access to research are not trade secrets. If the requested contracts contain genuinely sensitive financial figures that are legally protected, the TPIA requires you to redact those specific figures rather than withhold the entire document. I am willing to accept redacted documents provided the operational, technical, and data-privacy clauses are visible.

If you believe any portion of these records is exempt from disclosure, I remind you that under Section 552.301 of the Government Code, you must formally request a decision from the Texas Attorney General within 10 business days of receiving this request, and you must provide me with a copy of your written communication to the Attorney General.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael Veale

title: Agreements and Technical Policies regarding Google and Microsoft Indexing

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Agreements and Technical Policies regarding Google and Microsoft Indexing
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting copies of public records that relate to the relationship, agreements, and technical arrangements between the University of California Press (UC Press) and both Google LLC (including Alphabet Inc. and its subsidiaries) and Microsoft Corporation.

Specifically, I am seeking records concerning the indexing of academic content by their respective web crawlers (e.g., Googlebot, Google Scholar, Bingbot) and the bypassing of UC Press subscription paywalls.

Please provide the following recorded information:

1. Contracts and Agreements: Copies of any current, active contracts, Memorandums of Understanding (MOUs), or formal agreements between UC Press and Google, as well as UC Press and Microsoft, concerning the crawling, indexing, or full-text access of paywalled scholarly articles.
2. Technical Access Policies: Any internal policy documents, technical guidelines, or manuals detailing how UC Press (or its hosting partners, such as Silverchair) configures its servers or security systems to allow Google and Microsoft bots to bypass standard paywalls or CAPTCHAs (e.g., IP whitelisting protocols, "First Page" access rules, or the provision of specific metadata tags for search engine consumption).
3. Data Sharing: Any documentation outlining what user data, traffic analytics, or usage statistics (if any) UC Press provides to Google or Microsoft in exchange for these indexing services.

Pre-emptive arguments regarding CPRA Exemptions:

I anticipate that you may consider applying exemptions to this request, specifically regarding trade secrets or the "catch-all" exemption. I ask that you carefully consider the following points:

Regarding Trade Secrets (Evidence Code § 1060): While pricing structures may be exempt, the basic operational parameters, technical access rules, and data privacy termsa by which a public university press grants access to the world's largest search engines do not constitute exempt trade secrets. The general mechanisms of search engine indexing are widely understood; revealing the nature of your compliance with these mechanisms does not provide a competitive advantage to rival publishers.

Regarding the "Catch-All" Exemption (Gov. Code § 7922.000): This exemption requires that the public interest in withholding the records clearly outweighs the public interest in disclosure. The public interest in understanding how a public institution disseminates academic research and interacts with monopolistic tech companies is immense.

Duty to Segregate and Redact (Gov. Code § 7922.525): If the requested contracts contain genuinely sensitive financial figures that are legally exempt, the CPRA dictates that any reasonably segregable portion of a record shall be provided after deletion of the portions that are exempted. I am perfectly happy to receive heavily redacted agreements, provided the non-commercial clauses (such as those detailing technical access, data privacy, and general terms of indexing) are disclosed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael Veale

communications.subject: #26-0083: California Public Records Act Request: Agreements and Technical Policies regarding Google and Microsoft Indexing
communications.communication:
Dear Michael Veale:
This is to acknowledge your California Public Records Act (CPRA) request. Appropriate UC Office of the President offices are being notified of your request. Records identified as responsive to your request will be reviewed, and made available for your access, in accordance with relevant law and University policy. The estimated date of production for all new requests is eight weeks, though we are often able to provide records more quickly.

Please note that there is no central repository for all University records. The identification and collection of potentially responsive records is only the first step in the CPRA response process. The collected records must be reviewed by this office to ensure that they are in fact responsive to your request and to assess whether any legal privileges or exemptions from disclosure are applicable and whether redactions may be required to protect individuals’ rights to privacy. Please also bear in mind that we are concurrently fulfilling numerous other requests, many of which were received before yours and that we generally process requests in the order in which they are received.

Although the requested records have not yet been fully gathered and reviewed, it is possible that the requested material may contain information exempt from disclosure pursuant to the CPRA. However, this is not a determination that the requested records are necessarily exempt from disclosure. This office will provide you with a status update after the requested records have been thoroughly reviewed.

Inquiries about this CPRA request should refer to CPRA #26-0083.

Sincerely,

title: City of Watsonville: Photographs of Graffiti Uploaded to Watsonville city website

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: City of Watsonville: Photographs of Graffiti Uploaded to Watsonville city website
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All photographs of graffiti uploaded via the city of Watsonville's website. 

Time frame for request: June 1st 2023 to March 1st 2026. 

The photographs would have been uploaded using this form:

https://www.watsonville.gov/FormCenter/Public-Works-16/Report-Graffiti-106

This is a request for just the photographs. This is not a request for any other identifying information. 

Please provide any reasonable segregable portion of a requested record after removing or redacting those portions claimed to be exempt, explain in writing the justification for redacting the record, indicate the extent of any redactions on the portion of the record which is made available or published and where technically feasible indicate on the redacted portion itself the specific exemption(s) claimed. There is a public interest in seeing what has been flagged as graffiti by the public to the city of Watsonville. 

Thank you for the city of Watsonville's commitment to transparency.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

communications.subject: Your City of Watsonville, CA public records request #26-116 has been submitted.
communications.communication:
City of Watsonville, CA ************************************************************************ Your record request #26-116 has been submitted successfully. Dear Requester:  The City of Watsonville ("City") is in receipt of the Public Records Act request you submitted. The City intends to cooperate to the fullest extent possible with the Public Records Act ("PRA") (Government Code section 7920.000 et seq.) Under established California law, the City is obliged to comply with a request for a public record so long as the requestor makes a specific and focused request for information, that information is maintained by the City in its ordinary course of business, the information is disclosable, and the record can be located with reasonable effort. The PRA provides for the inspection or copying of existing identifiable public records; it does not compel the City to respond directly to questions or create new records, lists, or reports in response to a request. Within the next ten (10) days, we will determine whether your request seeks copies of disclosable records in the City's possession or whether the City will require an extension. communications.communication: Thank you for contacting the City of El Cajon. We have received your request for information and will begin processing it. A response to this request will be sent in a timely manner as allowed by California Government Code Section 7922.535.  In unusual circumstances, that time period may be extended an additional 14 days. We make every effort to respond to requests for information as quickly as possible. Normal business days are Monday - Thursday, 7:30am - 5:30pm, and every other Friday 8:00am - 5:00pm. For alternate dark Fridays, please check the City calendar available online. To check on the status of your request, return to the Public Records site and click on Lookup Request. *NOTE: At "Lookup Request", enter the tracking number, and click on the Submit button (Enter key will not work). You will be prompted for this tracking number, PRR26-201, along with your email, 206007-64844848@requests.muckrock.com

title: Female Slips Cuffs & Steals Officer’s Gun (Larimer County, CO – Feb 19, 2023)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Colorado Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Hello,
Under the Colorado Open Records Act, I am requesting the full, unedited body-worn camera (BWC) video(s) and any in-car/dashcam video(s) related to the incident in Larimer County, Colorado on/around February 19, 2023, where a female suspect slipped out of her handcuffs while seated in the rear of a patrol vehicle and took an officer’s firearm.

Please include:
	1.	All responding officers’ body camera videos from initial contact through the end of the incident (including transport, station/booking, and any interviews if recorded).
	2.	Any dispatch/radio audio associated with this incident.
	3.	The incident/case number and any related report numbers.

Delivery: Please provide the records electronically via email (or a secure download link sent to email).

If there are any fees, please provide a cost estimate before processing. If any portions are withheld or redacted, please provide the legal basis for the redactions/withholdings and release the remainder.

Thank you.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Unseen BodyCam Cases

Hi, thank you.
I’m requesting this footage for educational / informational purposes and I have a very limited budget.
Please provide a fee estimate before processing and do not proceed if costs will be high.
To keep costs as low as possible, I’m okay with a narrowed request. Please provide the lowest-cost option, such as:
	1.	Only the primary officer’s body-worn camera video directly involved in the incident (from first contact through the end),
	2.	If multiple cameras exist, please exclude all other officers’ bodycams unless necessary,
	3.	Include only dispatch/radio audio if it is bundled with no extra cost, otherwise omit it,
	4.	If available, send via email or a secure download link (preferred).
If any portion is withheld/redacted, please release the rest and cite the reason for the withheld parts.
Thank you.
